title: dactylorhiza incarnata var. baumgartneriana
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. baumgartneriana, also known as Baumgartner's orchid or the flesh-colored spotted orchid, is a rare and endemic species of orchid native to the Balkan Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Dactylorhiza incarnata var. baumgartneriana can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
